title: polygonum truellum
intro: Polygonum truellum, also known as Persian orache, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Polygonum truellum include its ability to reduce swelling and ease pain, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Polygonum truellum can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding the plant into a powder for use in capsules or tinctures.
